description: A point (symbol) map: one Circle mark per geographic member, placed on generated Lat/Long, sized by a measure. Distinct from a choropleth (which fills regions); use when magnitude is encoded by symbol size rather than region shading.

slots.notes: template column carries semantic-role='[Country].[ISO3166_2]'; the LOD level drives generated Lat/Long. Portability is 'across the committed fixture classes + render-verified', not a blanket 'any dataset'.
template column carries semantic-role='[City].[Name]'; finest geographic grain — one symbol per city.
aggregated measure driving symbol size (proportional-symbol encoding).

hazards.detail: Rows/Cols are [Latitude (generated)]/[Longitude (generated)] — Tableau auto-generates these ONLY when the bound LOD fields carry a recognized geographic semantic-role. Binding non-geo dimensions produces no map (empty generated coordinates).
A geo-slot bind proves role==dimension, NOT that the bound field geocodes; the sanity/render leg is the backstop (same residual as spatial-choropleth-map).
